item.title: About
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Most of what you'll find here right now was produced by Flynn, who's a tech evangelist at Buoyant, educating developers about Linkerd, Kubernetes, and cloud-native development in general. He has spent 40+ years in software engineering (from the kernel up through distributed applications, with a common thread of communications and security throughout), and is a coauthor of Linkerd: Up and Running from O'Reilly, a co-lead of the Gateway API GAMMA initiative, and the original author and longest-serving maintainer of the Emissary-ingress API gateway. He is most easily found as @flynn on the CNCF or Linkerd Slacks.

item.title: On Shipping Software
item.author: Unknown
item.description: There are two kinds of software: software that ships, and software that's irrelevant.

item.title: On Scaling Nodes and Startup Races
item.author: Unknown
item.description: What really happens when all your nodes die?

item.title: The Comment Manifesto
item.author: Unknown
item.description: If this code ships and your comments aren't in it, you'll regret it -- maybe not today, maybe not tomorrow, but soon, and for the rest of your life.
